disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A cluster of small firm dome-shaped bumps has been present in the right groin for about 4 months and was painless until now
trigger_question_examples: How long have the bumps been there?
Were the bumps bothering your child before today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They've been there about four months. They never seemed to bother my child at all until this red, sore flare-up started a couple of days ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Over the last 48 hours the skin around several bumps became acutely red, swollen and tender
trigger_question_examples: What changed in the last day or two?
Which parts are red and sore?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: In the last two days the skin around some of the bumps went bright red, a bit puffy and sore to touch. That's what scared me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On close inspection the older, uninflamed bumps each have a tiny central dimple/dent with a small whitish core
trigger_question_examples: If you look closely at the bumps that are not red, do any of them have a little dimple or dent in the middle?
Can you describe exactly what one of the older bumps looks like up close, or send a photo?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it - the ones that aren't red do have a tiny dent right in the centre, and there's a little whitish spot in the middle of them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Some bumps line up along scratch marks (linear autoinoculation pattern) and the child scratches the area
trigger_question_examples: Does your child scratch the bumps?
Are any of the bumps arranged in a line?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, my child scratches them, and some of the bumps seem to run in a little line where they've scratched.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The child is afebrile and systemically well - eating, drinking and playing normally with no lethargy
trigger_question_examples: Has your child had any fever?
Are they otherwise well in themselves - eating, drinking, active?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever at all - I checked with the thermometer and it was normal. They're eating, drinking and playing as usual, just limping a little because of the sore spot.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't taken a temperature, but they don't feel hot to me and they're behaving completely normally.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thick pus pocket, no rapidly spreading hot/hard redness down the leg, no red streak tracking up from the area
trigger_question_examples: Is there any pocket of thick yellow pus, or is the redness spreading quickly and feeling hot and hard?
Is there a red line or streak running away from the area up the leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No - there's no pus, and the redness is just around the bumps. It isn't racing down the leg and there's no red streak or line.
